metadata.title: AI Visual Synthesis — Complete Reference
metadata.description: Unified prompt engineering framework combining prompt templates, skill layers, and design vocabulary

quick_reference.ai_instructions: Before generating code, read SKILLS.md and apply the 8-Layer Architecture.

eight_layer_architecture.template: ROLE → CONTEXT → OBJECTIVE → CONSTRAINTS → AESTHETIC → PLANNING → OUTPUT → REFINEMENT

definitions.label: Portrait
definitions.description: Human subjects, character studies, facial expressions

skill_nodes.title: Technical Prompt Engineering
skill_nodes.subtitle: 6 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: Constructing prompts as structured blueprints — the interface between human intent and machine execution.

skills.name: The Scaffold Method
skills.description: Subject → Style → Technical → Mood → Negative — structured prompt layers for reproducible output.

skills.name: Universal Blueprint
skills.description: A subject [action/pose], [environment], [lighting], [camera], [style], [mood], [negative] template that works across all platforms.

skills.name: Front-Loading
skills.description: CLIP and T5 attention weights front tokens highest — always lead with your most critical descriptors.

skills.name: Keyword Weighting
skills.description: Use (keyword:1.3) for emphasis, [keyword:0.7] for de-emphasis. Stack modifiers to control semantic drift.

skills.name: Photographic Vocabulary
skills.description: 500+ curated style tokens mapped to real optical outcomes. Replace vague adjectives with measurable parameters.

skills.name: Agent Prompting Patterns
skills.description: Meta-prompts, chain-of-thought injection, and role-based system prompts for LLM-driven generation pipelines.

skill_nodes.title: Optical Physics & Depth Perception
skill_nodes.subtitle: 4 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: The foundation of all visual engineering — understanding how focal length governs psychological perception.

skills.name: Focal Length Mapping
skills.description: 14mm–800mm semantic map: 14mm exaggerates environment, 85mm flatters portraiture, 400mm compresses backgrounds.

skills.name: Center of Projection (COP)
skills.description: The mathematical origin of perspective — how COP distance determines whether subjects feel intimate, heroic, or detached.

skills.name: Inverse Square Law
skills.description: Light intensity falls off at 1/d². Understanding this is the difference between flat and dimensional lighting.

skills.name: Sensor Format Impact
skills.description: Full frame vs. crop vs. medium format: how sensor size affects depth of field, compression, and tonal rendering.

skill_nodes.title: Advanced Photographic Literacy
skill_nodes.subtitle: 5 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: Reconstructing real-world physics — the key to believable photorealism and tactile truth.

skills.name: Lighting Pattern Mastery
skills.description: Rembrandt, butterfly, loop, split, broad, short — each pattern creates a specific shadow map that communicates psychological intent.

skills.name: Lighting Ratios
skills.description: Key-to-fill ratios (1:1 flat → 8:1 dramatic) mapped to emotional register. The ratio is the mood.

skills.name: Color Temperature Control
skills.description: 2700K (candlelight) → 10000K (overcast shade) — precise Kelvin values as prompt tokens for scientifically accurate color casts.

skills.name: Aperture Control
skills.description: f/1.2 isolates, f/22 maximizes depth. Bokeh quality (circular vs. cat-eye) engineered through specific lens descriptors.

skills.name: Lens Selection Psychology
skills.description: Each optical design (spherical, anamorphic, tilt-shift) creates a distinct relationship between subject, environment, and viewer.

skill_nodes.title: Strategic Negation & Material Science
skill_nodes.subtitle: 5 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: Telling the AI what not to include — overcoming the uncanny valley through material physics.

skills.name: Negative Prompting with Weights
skills.description: Compound exclusion at weight ≥1.2: (blurry, deformed, plastic skin:1.4). Target artifacts specifically rather than generically.

skills.name: Skin Realism Management
skills.description: Subsurface scattering vocabulary, pore-level texture tokens, and specular highlight control for defeating the plastic-skin artifact.

skills.name: Subsurface Scattering (SSS)
skills.description: Light penetrates skin 2–3mm and exits at a different point. SSS prompts simulate this biological reality for tactile believability.

skills.name: Fresnel Reflectance
skills.description: Surfaces reflect more at grazing angles. Fresnel-aware prompts create physically accurate specularity on skin, glass, and wet surfaces.

skills.name: Anisotropic Reflections
skills.description: Directional micro-surface structure (hair, brushed metal, silk) creates stretched rather than circular highlights — critical for material convincingness.

skill_nodes.title: Identity Preservation & Consistency
skill_nodes.subtitle: 4 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: Maintaining specific character or style across multiple generations — critical for storytelling, brand work, and production pipelines.

skills.name: Seed Locking
skills.description: Fix the noise seed to maintain compositional consistency across iterations. Combine with cfg_scale variation for controlled exploration.

skills.name: Character Reference (--cref)
skills.description: Midjourney's --cref flag with --cw weight control: inject a reference image's identity into new generations at controlled strength.

skills.name: Style Reference (--sref)
skills.description: Separate style from content — apply an aesthetic reference without forcing the subject. --sref + --cref simultaneously for full control.

skills.name: Multi-Reference Consistency
skills.description: IP-Adapter + ControlNet + LoRA stacking in ComfyUI for cross-platform character consistency. Character sheets as reference anchors.

skill_nodes.title: Anamorphic Mastery & Cinematic Grammar
skill_nodes.subtitle: 4 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: Engineering narrative scale and texture through cylindrical optics — the hallmark of visual architects.

skills.name: Squeeze Factor Selection
skills.description: 1.33x (gentle widescreen) vs. 2.0x (extreme cinematic). Squeeze ratio determines oval bokeh geometry and horizontal flare character.

skills.name: Anamorphic Artifact Engineering
skills.description: Intentional horizontal lens flares (blue/amber), oval bokeh, and focus breathing — the physical signatures of cylindrical optics.

skills.name: De-Squeeze Workflow
skills.description: Generating in squeezed format then de-squeezing in post for authentic anamorphic results rather than simulated ones.

skills.name: Lens Breathing Control
skills.description: The focus-distance relationship unique to anamorphic designs — background scale shifts as focus changes, adding organic life.

skill_nodes.title: Post-Processing & Hybrid Workflows
skill_nodes.subtitle: 5 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: Treating AI generation as the beginning, not the end — refining outputs into professional assets.

skills.name: Inpainting
skills.description: Mask-based regional regeneration: fix hands, correct faces, replace backgrounds — surgical precision on specific areas.

skills.name: Outpainting
skills.description: Extend images beyond their original canvas. Used for aspect ratio changes, scene expansion, and contextual addition.

skills.name: Color Grading
skills.description: LUT application, Lightroom AI masking, and curves adjustment to unify AI-generated assets into a coherent visual language.

skills.name: External AI Enhancement
skills.description: Topaz Gigapixel (4x upscaling), ESRGAN (texture recovery), and Neat Video (noise reduction) — AI-to-AI refinement chains.

skills.name: Cross-Domain Compositing
skills.description: Combining AI-generated elements with photography, 3D renders, and motion graphics. Matching light, color, and perspective.

skill_nodes.title: AI Agent Orchestration
skill_nodes.subtitle: 4 SKILLS
skill_nodes.description: Designing and managing multi-step, autonomous workflows — the frontier of scalable AI creativity.

skills.name: Agent Architecture Design
skills.description: Planner → Researcher → Generator → Reviewer → Critic chains. Each role has a defined scope, tool access, and handoff protocol.

skills.name: Router Pattern Implementation
skills.description: Conditional logic that routes tasks to specialized sub-agents based on content type, complexity score, or output format required.

skills.name: Role-Based Agent Teams
skills.description: Art Director, Prompt Engineer, Quality Reviewer, and Style Consistency agents operating in parallel with a shared memory context.

skills.name: Cross-Agent Communication
skills.description: Structured message passing, shared vector memory, and validation gates between agents. Fallback protocols for degraded outputs.

core_effects.description: Frosted glass panels with translucent backdrop
Raw, oversized typography with high contrast
Single bright color pop against dark

advanced_effects.description: Bold shadows, flat colors, thick borders

architecture: Role defined — who is the AI acting as?
design: Platform specified — mobile or web or hybrid
workflow: Output from one feeds into next
